title: Boat Rental in Bodrum: Your 2026 Guide to Choosing the Right Vessel
html:
When you decide to rent a boat in Bodrum, starting from the bustling Marina Vita or the quieter D-Marin Turgutreis, daily rates typically range from 1,500 TRY for a small motorboat to 25,000 TRY for a crewed luxury yacht. BoatHire24 connects you straight to verified owners so you can compare real options and browse boats for your dates across this stunning stretch of the Aegean Sea.

Where to set off in Bodrum

Bodrum offers several well-equipped marinas and sheltered bays that serve as excellent departure points. The main harbour at Bodrum Marina sits right beneath the castle, with ample berths and fuelling facilities. D-Marin Turgutreis, about 20 kilometres west, is popular for its yacht services and easy access to the islands. Further south, the small fishing harbour at Yalıkavak is a gateway to the open sea, while Palmiye Marina in Gümbet provides a calmer start for less experienced skippers. From any of these, you can plan a route that takes in quiet coves such as Aquarium Bay or longer passages past the Greek island of Kos, roughly 12 nautical miles from Bodrum.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists dozens of vessel types in Bodrum, each suited to a different style of day out. The list below gives a quick overview of the most popular options.

  1. Motorboat (5, 7 m), Ideal for a small group wanting to explore nearby bays at their own pace; accommodates 4, 6 guests; from 1,500 TRY half-day.
  2. RIB (Rigid Inflatable Boat), Fast and nimble for watersports or island hops; fits 6, 8 people; from 2,500 TRY for a four-hour session.
  3. Sailing yacht (30, 40 ft), Perfect for a full-day calm cruise with a light breeze; sleeps up to 6 overnight; from 5,000 TRY a day bareboat.
  4. Catamaran (38, 45 ft), Spacious and stable for a family or two couples; carries 8, 12 guests; from 8,000 TRY full-day with a skipper.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (Azimut, Sunseeker, 50, 60 ft), A crewed experience with comforts such as a flybridge and cabin; up to 12 guests; from 12,000 TRY per day.

For a practical example, consider a half-day rental aboard a QuickSilver Activ 605 Open powered by a 150 HP engine. It zips around Bodrum’s coastline at a lively pace and can be booked through this speedboat listing that shows exact availability and terms.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Bodrum?

Rental prices in Bodrum vary by vessel type, season, and duration, with daily rates from about 1,500 TRY to 25,000 TRY. To give you a clearer picture, here is a typical breakdown:

  • Shared group tour (gulet, full-day, lunch included), from 800 TRY per person.
  • Small motorboat (5 m, no skipper, half-day), from 1,500 TRY.
  • Catamaran (40 ft, with skipper, full-day), from 8,000 TRY.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (60 ft, all inclusive, full-day), from 15,000 TRY.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Bodrum?

For most small motorboats and sailing yachts under a certain size, a short local licence or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is required. Turkish regulations for 2026 still demand that any rental without a skipper be operated by someone holding a valid boating licence. The specific horsepower and length thresholds can change, so always check with your host before booking. A good rule is that if the boat has more than 15 HP or is longer than 7 m, a licence is almost certainly needed. The operators on BoatHire24 list these requirements clearly, and you can message them for exact confirmation.

When exploring the waters around Bodrum in August, be aware of the meltemi wind that can pick up suddenly in the afternoon. Start early, avoid the heat of the day, and keep an eye on the horizon for whitecaps.

At a glance

  • Price range: 1,500 TRY, 25,000 TRY per day
  • Peak season: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Maximum guests without skipper: 6 (most small motorboats)
  • Currency: Turkish Lira (TRY)

Questions about boat rental in Bodrum

Can I rent a boat in Bodrum without a licence and with a skipper?

Yes, the majority of boat owners offer a skipper included in the price, so you can relax and enjoy the scenery without any paperwork.

What is the best month to rent a boat in Bodrum?

May, June, and September provide warm, calm seas and fewer crowds, making them ideal months for a charter in 2026.

Is it safe to sail from Bodrum to the Greek islands?

It is safe provided you have the correct documentation, but you must clear customs and passport control before arriving in Greece; your skipper can advise on the formalities.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Bodrum?

For July and August, it is wise to book at least two weeks ahead; outside peak season, a week is often enough to secure your preferred vessel.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bodrum or list your own. Whether you are planning a half-day family outing or a full-week luxury cruise, the platform makes it simple to compare options and book with confidence. Take a look at the current choices and browse the listings to find your perfect vessel for 2026. If you own a boat in the region, you can also become a host and start earning from your asset.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bodrum and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
